why_them: Barlochan House runs a Next.js/WordPress hybrid built on 'Flexi Blocks Builder', a niche proprietary stack that creates long-term vendor dependency. Several template variables are still unfilled at the code level — the brochure download button renders a literal '%%BROCHURE_URL%%' placeholder and the Care Inspectorate link is null, meaning live visitors see a broken download. Social media icons are hidden via inline CSS ('display:none'), suggesting they were set up but never activated. The site has no active Care Inspectorate badge populated.
recommended_service: Content audit and technical clean-up, social media activation and management, SEO optimisation
outreach_hook: Your brochure download button on barlochanhouse.co.uk is showing a broken placeholder link right now — visitors clicking it get nothing. That's an easy fix, and while we're in there we spotted a couple of other quick wins worth showing you.

why_them: Bankfoot House uses Squarespace, which limits custom functionality and SEO depth compared to a bespoke WordPress build. The fees page references 'May 2025' as if it is current, suggesting content hasn't been reviewed in nearly a year. There is no Google Maps embed anywhere on the site, which is a significant usability gap for families trying to locate a care home in an unfamiliar area. No online booking or virtual tour capability exists. As a registered charity with a strong local reputation, they would benefit from digital storytelling and a Google Business profile strategy.
recommended_service: Squarespace-to-WordPress migration, local SEO, Google Maps integration, content refresh
outreach_hook: Families searching for a care home in Moffat can't find a map on bankfoothouse.com — one of the most-expected features — which likely costs you enquiries every week.

why_them: Senwick House's site is built on Toolkit Websites, a low-end templated platform that severely limits SEO performance and modern UX. COVID-19 visiting guidance is still live on the site — over three years after restrictions ended — which signals to families that the site is not actively maintained. Footer 'Terms' and 'Cookies' links are dead placeholder anchors ('#'), which is a GDPR compliance risk. The mobile navigation collapses at an unusually wide 1200px breakpoint, meaning most tablet users see a hamburger menu instead of full navigation. No social media presence, no Google Maps, and no online enquiry form are visible.
recommended_service: Full website redesign off Toolkit Websites, GDPR compliance fixes, local SEO, social media setup
outreach_hook: Senwick House's website still has COVID visiting guidance on it, and the Terms and Cookies links in the footer go nowhere — both are the kind of thing families and regulators notice.

location: Gatehouse of Fleet, Castle Douglas, Dumfries & Galloway
why_them: Fleet Valley runs WordPress with the Avada theme and an outdated LayerSlider v6.11.2 — a plugin with known security vulnerabilities in older versions. A COVID-19 care page is still present in the navigation. There are no social media links anywhere in the footer. Google Analytics is tracking on the universal UA- prefix (UA-177006852-1) rather than GA4, meaning analytics data will have stopped flowing since the UA sunset in 2023. No Google Maps embed is visible despite serving visitors from a relatively remote location.
recommended_service: WordPress security audit and plugin updates, GA4 migration, SEO, social media management
outreach_hook: Fleet Valley's Google Analytics stopped recording data in 2023 because the site is still on the old UA tracking code — you've had a blind spot on your web traffic for two years.

why_them: Dryfemount has a relatively modern WordPress site but lacks a Google Maps embed despite serving a rural area where families often travel significant distances. No staff directory or care team profiles are present, which is a notable trust-building gap in the care home sector. The site has a sister home suggesting a small group operator with some budget capacity. Social media is limited to a single Facebook page. There is no enquiry or waitlist form — only a general contact page — which may reduce conversion from organic traffic.
recommended_service: Google Maps integration, content strategy (staff profiles, testimonials), enquiry form optimisation, SEO
outreach_hook: Dryfemount's site looks good but has no map and no staff profiles — two things families specifically look for when choosing a care home.

why_them: West View's website was last substantively updated in September 2016 — nearly a decade ago — and is built on ProSiteHosting's proprietary website builder, a platform that has effectively ceased development. The site has a Google Maps API key embedded in the code but no map is rendered anywhere, meaning it was half-implemented and never finished. There is no email address provided to the public anywhere on the site. Social media fields exist in the template but are empty. A small 12-bed independent home in a beautiful coastal location has a compelling story to tell that its current site completely fails to communicate.
recommended_service: Full website rebuild, Google Maps integration, local SEO, email contact setup, Google Business profile
outreach_hook: West View's website has been sitting unchanged since 2016 — the platform it's built on no longer exists in any meaningful way, and there's no email address for families to reach you.

location: Old Minnigaff, Newton Stewart, Dumfries & Galloway
why_them: Cumloden Manor's website is built on BaseKit/ProSiteHosting — last meaningfully updated in August 2016 — and contains visible template placeholder text ('Nullam Posuer') in the footer, which is Latin filler copy that was never replaced, visible to any visitor. The site has a Google Maps API key hardcoded but renders no map. Several image URLs still use insecure http:// rather than https://, which triggers browser security warnings. The inspection report link points to a Dropbox PDF from an unknown date with no verification it is current. No social media profiles are linked despite fields existing.
recommended_service: Full website rebuild, HTTPS migration, Google Maps integration, local SEO, Google Business profile management
outreach_hook: Cumloden Manor's website footer still has 'Nullam Posuer' — Latin placeholder text that was never replaced — visible to every family visiting your site right now.

why_them: Queensberry Care Home in Sanquhar has no dedicated website whatsoever — families searching online find only third-party directory listings on HousingCare, Care Choices, and the Care Inspectorate. The home provides nursing care for up to 42 residents including dementia care, representing a mid-sized independent facility with no digital presence of its own. Without a website, the home cannot control its online reputation, showcase its environment, or generate enquiries directly.
recommended_service: New website design and build, Google Business profile, local SEO, social media setup
outreach_hook: Queensberry Care Home doesn't appear anywhere online under its own name — families searching for care in Sanquhar find directory listings but no website, no story, and no way to contact you directly.
